school_request__special_circumstances.special_circumstances:
I provide daily after-school care for Ethan and Sophia Reynolds at my home because both of their parents, Daniel and Laura Reynolds, work full-time and have variable schedules. Daniel is a respiratory therapist at St. Joseph’s Hospital in Syracuse, and he often works 12-hour shifts that can extend into the evening. Laura is an assistant manager at a retail store in Destiny USA and frequently has closing shifts, which means she is not able to be home at dismissal time.

The children’s home address is in the Clay area, which typically routes them to a different elementary school and bus pattern. However, my residence on Brookhaven Drive is within the Chestnut Hill Elementary School attendance area, and it is where the children go every school day immediately after school. They are with me Monday through Friday, generally from school dismissal until around 6:30–7:00 PM, and sometimes later if one of the parents is held over at work due to staffing shortages or inclement weather.

Because of the parents’ work schedules, it is not practical or safe for the children to ride a bus that goes near their home in Clay and then have someone else transport them to my house in Liverpool afterward. There is no other adult consistently available in the Clay neighborhood during those hours, and both parents leave for work before the typical dismissal time. Additionally, during the winter months, driving conditions can be poor, and having the children transported multiple times between locations increases their risk and the chance for confusion or missed pick-ups.

I have been watching Ethan and Sophia informally since Sophia was about three years old, and more formally after school since the beginning of the 2020–2021 school year, when the pandemic significantly disrupted both parents’ schedules. The children have an established routine at my home: they get off the bus, have a snack, complete homework at the dining room table, and then have structured playtime either in the living room or, when weather permits, in the backyard. I also provide a quiet space for Ethan, who sometimes needs a calmer environment to focus on reading and math assignments. His teacher last year noted that having a consistent after-school routine at my house helped improve his homework completion and overall performance.

There are no other nearby relatives who can reliably assist. The grandparents live in Utica and are only able to help on occasional weekends. The parents explored licensed daycare centers and after-school programs, but the waitlists were long, the hours did not consistently cover their evening shifts, and the cost for two children was prohibitive, especially considering the possibility of frequent schedule changes.

For these reasons, we are requesting that the children be allowed to ride the bus that serves my address so that they can be dropped off at or near 214 Brookhaven Drive each school day. This arrangement ensures that they are supervised immediately after school by a consistent caregiver, reduces transportation complexity and risk, and supports the parents’ ability to maintain their employment. I am prepared to continue providing this care for the full 2021–2022 school year and, if needed, beyond, and I will notify the district immediately if there are any changes to the arrangement, such as my moving or no longer being able to watch the children.
